scenario: Fork detection: two successors claim the same lineage
description: Agent A terminates. Both A' and A'' present valid-looking rotation proofs claiming succession from A. Expected: identity layer flags ambiguity, trust score frozen (neither decayed nor accrued) until external resolution.

expected.identity_verdict_reason: Two valid rotation signatures reference the same parent key. APS spec requires exactly one successor per rotation event.
expected.trust_behavior: Trust score locked at pre-fork value until ambiguity resolves. Neither claimant accrues trust. Delegator-side reputation queries return FROZEN status with both candidates listed.
resolution_paths: Predecessor out-of-band attestation designates the legitimate successor.
External arbiter (delegator, registry, or governance body) elects one claimant.
Both claimants voluntarily withdraw and a new rotation is initiated.
Timeout after N days without resolution collapses relationship to revoked.
expected.handoff_count_note: Handoff is not considered complete until ambiguity resolves.

aip13_delegationChain.note: Delegation chain resolution MUST block on ambiguous rotation. Accepting actions from either claimant before resolution risks double-authorization.

erc8004_keyRotation.note: Fork events SHOULD be anchored. On-chain audit trail is how downstream peers detect they need to suspend interactions with either claimant.
